channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Fox News Politics: Crunch time for Harris
item.description: Get the latest updates from the 2024 campaign trail, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Carolina early voters, still recovering from Helene, exceed 2020 voter turnout
item.description: The number of people casting ballots on the first day of early in-person voting in presidential battleground North Carolina exceeded the first-day total four years ago — even as Hurricane Helene recovery continued in the mountains.
item.dc:creator: Gary D. Robertson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House Dems sue FEC accusing GOP of exploiting campaign loophole
item.description: The Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee, the House Democrats campaign arm, filed its lawsuit against the FEC on Thursday, alleging Republicans are using a campaign finance loophole in their television ads.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nevada Senate hopefuls tackle trans athletes, immigration and UFOs in only debate
item.description: Sen. Jackie Rosen and Republican candidate Sam Brown went head to head in the Nevada Senate debate on immigration, the economy and biological males competing in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats look to quash threat from Green Party's Jill Stein
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over Green Party candidate Jill Stein as they look to avoid a repeat of the 2016 presidential election, in which Stein was accused of playing spoiler in key swing states. For months, Democrats had trained their ire on Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Early voting begins in Louisiana, Hawaii, Washington
item.description: Louisiana, Hawaii and Washington begin early voting on Friday, joining the vast majority of U.S. states in kicking off the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As campaign nears finish, Walz adds dashes of digs to his portions of joy
item.description: The Democratic vice presidential candidate entered the race emphasizing positivity in politics. In the final stretch to Election Day, he’s trying to thread the needle between needling Donald Trump and JD Vance while still keeping an optimistic message.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Nevada Senate debate
item.description: Sen. Jacky Rosen (D-Nev.) and Republican Sam Brown duked it out during their first and only debate Thursday night, making their pitches to voters in one of the key battleground states on the 2024 map and a must-win state for Democratic Senate majority hopes.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris and Trump seek Arab American votes in Michigan in effort to shore up battleground states
item.description: Kamala Harris and Donald Trump are both seeking support from Arab American voters as they campaign in Michigan, trying to lock down support in a battleground state that could decide the presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Following death of Hamas leader, Harris says it's 'time for the day after to begin' in Gaza
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Kamala Harris said Thursday that Israel's killing of Hamas' top leader offers "an opportunity to finally end the war in Gaza," talking plainly about next steps even as the pro-Palestinian demonstrators who gathered outside her campaign event underscored the complicated politics at p
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, COLLEEN LONG and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At Wisconsin stops, Harris campaign features less talk of joy and more attacks on Trump
item.description: In La Crosse on Thursday, Harris told a rally crowd that Trump “will stop at nothing to claim unchecked power for himself.” It’s a shift from the “joy” that swirled around her elevation to the top of the Democratic ticket this summer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Lydia Dominguez talks leadership, safety, and accountability in school board run for District B
item.description: Lydia Dominguez, candidate for the Clark County School Board in District B, joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to outline her vision for the district, focusing on financial accountability, student safety, and parental involvement.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Candidate Karl Catarata talks education, safety, and accountability in Clark County School Board race
item.description: Karl Catarata, running for the District A trustee seat on the Clark County School Board, joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide for an interview to discuss his platform and vision for the district.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma politicians debate ethics of 'paddling' students
item.description: There were 351 reported instances of Oklahoma students with disabilities who were stricken in schools during the 2020-21 school year.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation

item.title: Early voting begins in North Carolina, officials helping after Helene
item.description: With the election less than three weeks away, many residents are still reeling from Helene. This has led to some drastic changes to the voting process.
item.dc:creator: Devan Markham, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell privately slammed Trump as ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ after 2020 election
item.description: McConnell also criticized the former president as a “narcissist” and admitted after the 2020 election that “it’s not just the Democrats who are counting the days” until he left office.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: How to watch the Nevada US Senate Debate
item.description: The only debate between Democratic incumbent Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican challenger Sam Brown will occur on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Question 3: 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide
item.description: Dr. Sandra Cosgrove and Kerry Durmick joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to provide a proponent and opponent's view of one of the most contentious issues on the Nevada ballot in November: Question 3.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump uses interview on economics to promote tariffs and riff on his favorite themes
item.description: Donald Trump has seized on an opening to sound his frequent argument that imposing huge tariffs on foreign goods would amount to an economic elixir — one he claims would raise enormous sums for the government, protect U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump town hall ends with extended 'musical-fest' while he stands onstage
item.description: At a town hall in Oaks, Pa., on Monday, Trump ended the Q&A portion to ask the audience to remain and listen to music with him.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Lisa Cole, Nevada Assembly District 4 candidate, discusses economic development, affordable housing, and education
item.description: Lisa Cole, a candidate for Nevada Assembly District 4, appeared on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate rankings: 5 seats most likely to flip
item.description: The battle for the Senate majority has hit its final month as Republicans race to end their four-year stint in the minority. The GOP needs to win only one of two competitive races in red states — Montana and Ohio — to do just that, barring a surprise loss elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Johnson: Additional hurricane aid 'can wait' until Congress is back in session
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday said passing additional hurricane aid for states impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton “can wait” until Congress is back in session after the election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump hears at a Latino campaign event from someone who lived in the US illegally
item.description: Minutes after Donald Trump delivered his standard warnings about drug dealers and criminals illegally crossing the border during a Saturday campaign event, the former president heard from someone who was once in the country illegally but now plans to vote for him.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5
